95:   # https://github.com/KyleMayes/clang-sys/issues/213

97:   '{% if os() == "windows" %}libclang_bin="$(dirname "$MLN_FFI_DEPENDENCY_LIBRARY_DIR")/bin"; cp "$libclang_bin/libclang-13.dll" "$libclang_bin/libclang.dll"{% endif %}',
98:   "uv sync --locked --quiet",
99:   "pnpm install --frozen-lockfile --loglevel warn",
100:   "dotnet tool restore --tool-manifest dotnet-tools.json --verbosity quiet",
101:   "dotnet tool restore --tool-manifest bindings/dotnet/dotnet-tools.json --verbosity quiet",
102:   # Astro type stubs for vp typeCheck on docs sources (.astro/ is gitignored).
103:   "pnpm --filter docs exec astro sync",
104: ]
105: 
106: [tasks.configure]
107: depends = ["//:deps"]
108: run = [
109:   '''
110:   cmake_args=(
111:     -S .
112:     -B "$MLN_FFI_BUILD_DIR"
113:     -G Ninja
114:     -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE="$MLN_FFI_CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE"
115:     -DMLN_FFI_RENDER_BACKEND="$MLN_FFI_RENDER_BACKEND"
116:     -DMLN_FFI_OPENGL_CONTEXT_PROVIDER="$MLN_FFI_OPENGL_CONTEXT_PROVIDER"
117:     -DMLN_FFI_EGL_ROOT="$MLN_FFI_EGL_ROOT"
118:   )
119: 
120:   while IFS= read -r cmake_arg; do
121:     [[ -n "$cmake_arg" ]] && cmake_args+=("$cmake_arg")
122:   done <<< "${MLN_FFI_CMAKE_ARGS:-}"
123: 
124:   cmake "${cmake_args[@]}"
125:   ''',
126:   '''
127:   if [[ -f "$MLN_FFI_BUILD_DIR/compile_commands.json" ]]; then
128:     mkdir -p build/clangd
129:     compile_commands_link="build/clangd/compile_commands.json"
130:     compile_commands_tmp="${compile_commands_link}.tmp"
131:     rm -f "$compile_commands_tmp"
132:     ln -s "$MLN_FFI_BUILD_DIR/compile_commands.json" "$compile_commands_tmp"
133:     mv -f "$compile_commands_tmp" "$compile_commands_link"
134:   fi
135:   ''',
136: ]
137: 
138: [tasks.build]
139: depends = ["//:configure"]
140: run = '''
141: build_args=(
142:   --build "$MLN_FFI_BUILD_DIR"
143:   --parallel
144: )
145: 
146: cmake "${build_args[@]}"'''
147: 
148: [tasks.clean]
149: run = "rm -rf \"$MLN_FFI_BUILD_DIR\""
150: 
151: [tasks.deps]
152: description = "Install variant-specific managed dependencies."
153: run = '''
154: if [[ "$MLN_FFI_TARGET_TRIPLE" == "macos-arm64" ]]; then
155:   mise run deps:angle
156: fi
157: '''
158: 
159: [tasks.test]
160: depends = ["//:build"]
161: run = '''
162: (
163:   cd src/c_api/tests/zig
164:   zig build test {{ env.MLN_FFI_ZIG_BUILD_ARGS }} --test-timeout "$MLN_FFI_TEST_TIMEOUT" --summary all --verbose
165: )
166: (
167:   cd bindings/zig
168:   zig build test {{ env.MLN_FFI_ZIG_BUILD_ARGS }} --test-timeout "$MLN_FFI_TEST_TIMEOUT" --summary all --verbose
169: )
170: '''
171: 
172: [tasks.check]
173: run = "hk check --all"
174: 
175: [tasks.fix]
176: run = "hk fix --all"
177: 
178: [tasks.actions-up]
179: description = "Update and pin GitHub Actions steps."
180: run = "pnpm dlx actions-up"
181: 
182: [tasks._ktfmt]
183: description = "Called by dprint.jsonc"
184: hide = true
185: run = 'java -jar {{ tools["aqua:facebook/ktfmt"].path }}/ktfmt'
186: # ktfmt on windows doesn't work in git bash for some reason
187: shell = '{% if os() == "windows" %}cmd /c{% else %}sh -c -o errexit{% endif %}'
188: 
189: [tasks."ci:lint"]
190: run = [
191:   # Warm process-plugin cache before hk runs parallel dprint batches.
192:   "dprint output-resolved-config > /dev/null",
193:   "hk fix --all",
194:   "git update-index -q --refresh",
195:   # Lockfile drift is common across different mise builds
196:   "git diff --exit-code -- . ':(exclude)mise.lock'",
197:   "git diff --cached --exit-code -- . ':(exclude)mise.lock'",
198: ]
199: 
200: [tasks."ci:vulkan-info"]
201: description = "Print the active Vulkan loader and driver summary."
202: run = "vulkaninfo --summary"

# https://mise.jdx.dev
# Mise is the contributor entrypoint: it pins tools, installs hooks, and runs
# repository tasks.

experimental_monorepo_root = true

[settings]
lockfile = true
lockfile_platforms = ["linux-x64", "linux-arm64", "macos-arm64", "windows-x64"]
pin = true
experimental = true
python.uv_venv_auto = "create|source"
windows_default_file_shell_args = '"C:/Program Files/Git/bin/bash.exe" -e -c "source ""$MLN_FFI_REPO_ROOT/.mise/bin/windows-msvc-path.sh""; exec ""C:/Program Files/Git/bin/bash.exe"" ""$@""" _'
windows_default_inline_shell_args = '"C:/Program Files/Git/bin/bash.exe" -e -c "source ""$MLN_FFI_REPO_ROOT/.mise/bin/windows-msvc-path.sh""; eval ""$1""" _'
unix_default_file_shell_args = "bash"
unix_default_inline_shell_args = "bash -e -lc"
# On Windows, the Microsoft Store Python alias in WindowsApps can appear before
# mise-managed Python. Keep mise tool paths first so file tasks run pinned tools.
activate_aggressive = true

display.text:
# https://mise.jdx.dev
# Mise is the contributor entrypoint: it pins tools, installs hooks, and runs
# repository tasks.

experimental_monorepo_root = true

[settings]
lockfile = true
lockfile_platforms = ["linux-x64", "linux-arm64", "macos-arm64", "windows-x64"]
pin = true
experimental = true
python.uv_venv_auto = "create|source"
windows_default_file_shell_args = '"C:/Program Files/Git/bin/bash.exe" -e -c "source ""$MLN_FFI_REPO_ROOT/.mise/bin/windows-msvc-path.sh""; exec ""C:/Program Files/Git/bin/bash.exe"" ""$@""" _'
windows_default_inline_shell_args = '"C:/Program Files/Git/bin/bash.exe" -e -c "source ""$MLN_FFI_REPO_ROOT/.mise/bin/windows-msvc-path.sh""; eval ""$1""" _'
unix_default_file_shell_args = "bash"
unix_default_inline_shell_args = "bash -e -lc"
# On Windows, the Microsoft Store Python alias in WindowsApps can appear before
# mise-managed Python. Keep mise tool paths first so file tasks run pinned tools.
activate_aggressive = true

[monorepo]
config_roots = [".devcontainer", "bindings/*", "examples/*", "docs"]

[tools]
# General tooling
"aqua:jdx/usage" = "3.3.0" # CLI completion for mise tasks

"aqua:apple/pkl" = "0.31.0" # hk config dependency

"aqua:koalaman/shellcheck" = "0.11.0" # Shell script linter (used by actionlint)

"aqua:Kitware/CMake" = "4.3.3"
"aqua:ninja-build/ninja" = "1.13.2"

"aqua:XcodesOrg/xcodes" = { version = "1.6.2", os = ["macos"] }

# Node.js ecosystem
"core:node" = "24.11.0"
"aqua:pnpm/pnpm" = "10.33.2"

# Java/Kotlin ecosystem
"core:java" = "temurin-25.0.3+9.0.LTS"
"aqua:facebook/ktfmt" = "0.62"
"github:google/google-java-format" = "1.35.0"

# Python ecosystem
"core:python" = "3.14.4"
"aqua:astral-sh/uv" = "0.11.11"

# Swift ecosystem
"core:swift" = { version = "6.3.1", os = ["macos"] }
"github:nicklockwood/SwiftFormat" = "0.61.1"

# Other ecosystems
"core:rust" = { version = "1.95.0", profile = "minimal", components = "rustfmt,clippy" }

"core:go" = "1.26.3"
"core:dotnet" = "10.0.203"

[env]
UV_PYTHON = { value = "{{ tools.python.path }}", tools = true }
MLN_FFI_REPO_ROOT = "{{config_root}}"
MLN_FFI_VARIANT = { required = true }

MLN_FFI_RENDER_BACKEND = { required = true }

MLN_FFI_VULKAN_INCLUDE_DIR = "{{config_root}}/third_party/maplibre-native/vendor/Vulkan-Headers/include"

MLN_FFI_ZIG_TARGET = { required = true }

MLN_FFI_TEST_TIMEOUT = "{{env.MLN_FFI_TEST_TIMEOUT | default(value=\"30s\")}}"
CMAKE_PREFIX_PATH = "{{config_root}}/.pixi/envs/default"
PKG_CONFIG_PATH = "{{config_root}}/.pixi/envs/default/lib/pkgconfig"
LIBCLANG_PATH = "{{config_root}}/.pixi/envs/default/lib"

[hooks]
postinstall = [
  { task = "sync-submodules" },
  "hk install --mise --quiet",
  "pixi install --locked --quiet",
  # conda-forge ships libclang-13.dll, but clang-sys on Windows searches for libclang.dll.
  # https://github.com/KyleMayes/clang-sys/issues/213
  # https://github.com/conda-forge/llvmdev-feedstock/issues/376
  '{% if os() == "windows" %}libclang_bin="$(dirname "$MLN_FFI_DEPENDENCY_LIBRARY_DIR")/bin"; cp "$libclang_bin/libclang-13.dll" "$libclang_bin/libclang.dll"{% endif %}',
  "uv sync --locked --quiet",
  "pnpm install --frozen-lockfile --loglevel warn",
  "dotnet tool restore --tool-manifest dotnet-tools.json --verbosity quiet",
  "dotnet tool restore --tool-manifest bindings/dotnet/dotnet-tools.json --verbosity quiet",
  # Astro type stubs for vp typeCheck on docs sources (.astro/ is gitignored).
  "pnpm --filter docs exec astro sync",
]

[tasks.configure]
depends = ["//:deps"]
run = [
  '''
  cmake_args=(
    -S .
    -B "$MLN_FFI_BUILD_DIR"
    -G Ninja
    -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE="$MLN_FFI_CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE"
    -DMLN_FFI_RENDER_BACKEND="$MLN_FFI_RENDER_BACKEND"
    -DMLN_FFI_OPENGL_CONTEXT_PROVIDER="$MLN_FFI_OPENGL_CONTEXT_PROVIDER"
    -DMLN_FFI_EGL_ROOT="$MLN_FFI_EGL_ROOT"
  )

  while IFS= read -r cmake_arg; do
    [[ -n "$cmake_arg" ]] && cmake_args+=("$cmake_arg")
  done <<< "${MLN_FFI_CMAKE_ARGS:-}"

  cmake "${cmake_args[@]}"
  ''',
  '''
  if [[ -f "$MLN_FFI_BUILD_DIR/compile_commands.json" ]]; then
    mkdir -p build/clangd
    compile_commands_link="build/clangd/compile_commands.json"
    compile_commands_tmp="${compile_commands_link}.tmp"
    rm -f "$compile_commands_tmp"
    ln -s "$MLN_FFI_BUILD_DIR/compile_commands.json" "$compile_commands_tmp"
    mv -f "$compile_commands_tmp" "$compile_commands_link"
  fi
  ''',
]

[tasks.build]
depends = ["//:configure"]
run = '''
build_args=(
  --build "$MLN_FFI_BUILD_DIR"
  --parallel
)

cmake "${build_args[@]}"'''

[tasks.clean]
run = "rm -rf \"$MLN_FFI_BUILD_DIR\""

[tasks.deps]
description = "Install variant-specific managed dependencies."
run = '''
if [[ "$MLN_FFI_TARGET_TRIPLE" == "macos-arm64" ]]; then
  mise run deps:angle
fi
'''

[tasks.test]
depends = ["//:build"]
run = '''
(
  cd src/c_api/tests/zig
  zig build test {{ env.MLN_FFI_ZIG_BUILD_ARGS }} --test-timeout "$MLN_FFI_TEST_TIMEOUT" --summary all --verbose
)
(
  cd bindings/zig
  zig build test {{ env.MLN_FFI_ZIG_BUILD_ARGS }} --test-timeout "$MLN_FFI_TEST_TIMEOUT" --summary all --verbose
)
'''

[tasks.check]
run = "hk check --all"

[tasks.fix]
run = "hk fix --all"

[tasks.actions-up]
description = "Update and pin GitHub Actions steps."
run = "pnpm dlx actions-up"

[tasks._ktfmt]
description = "Called by dprint.jsonc"
hide = true
run = 'java -jar {{ tools["aqua:facebook/ktfmt"].path }}/ktfmt'
# ktfmt on windows doesn't work in git bash for some reason
shell = '{% if os() == "windows" %}cmd /c{% else %}sh -c -o errexit{% endif %}'

[tasks."ci:lint"]
run = [
  # Warm process-plugin cache before hk runs parallel dprint batches.
  "dprint output-resolved-config > /dev/null",
  "hk fix --all",
  "git update-index -q --refresh",
  # Lockfile drift is common across different mise builds
  "git diff --exit-code -- . ':(exclude)mise.lock'",
  "git diff --cached --exit-code -- . ':(exclude)mise.lock'",
]

[tasks."ci:vulkan-info"]
description = "Print the active Vulkan loader and driver summary."
run = "vulkaninfo --summary"

<path>/app/hk.pkl</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: // hk orchestrates checks for pre-commit, `mise run check`, and `mise run fix`.
2: // Tools used here must be installed by `mise install`.
3: 
4: amends "package://github.com/jdx/hk/releases/download/v1.45.0/hk@1.45.0#/Config.pkl"
5: 
6: import "package://github.com/jdx/hk/releases/download/v1.45.0/hk@1.45.0#/Builtins.pkl"
7: 
8: local lintSteps = new Mapping<String, Step> {
9:   ["dprint"] = (Builtins.dprint) {
10:     // the default batch=false hits the cmd length limit on windows
11:     batch = true
12:   }
13:   ["actionlint"] {
14:     glob = List(".github/workflows/*.yml", ".github/workflows/*.yaml")
15:     check = "actionlint {{files}}"
16:   }
17:   ["jvl"] {
18:     glob = List("**/*.json", "**/*.jsonc")
19:     check = "jvl check {{files}}"
20:   }
21:   // Mirrors Builtins.`vp-check` (available in hk 1.46+). dprint owns formatting.
22:   ["vp-check"] {
23:     glob = List(
24:       "**/*.js",
25:       "**/*.mjs",
26:       "**/*.cjs",
27:       "**/*.ts",
28:       "**/*.mts",
29:       "**/*.cts",
30:       "**/*.jsx",
31:       "**/*.tsx",
32:       "**/*.vue",
33:       "**/*.svelte",
34:       "**/*.astro",
35:     )
36:     exclude = List("third_party/**")
37:     check = "pnpm exec vp check --no-fmt {{files}}"
38:     fix = "pnpm exec vp check --no-fmt --fix {{files}}"
39:   }
40:   ["ruff"] {
41:     glob = List("**/*.py")
42:     exclude = List("third_party/**")
43:     check = "uv run ruff check {{files}} && uv run ruff format --check {{files}}"
44:     fix = "uv run ruff check --fix {{files}} && uv run ruff format {{files}}"
45:   }
46:   ["ty"] {
47:     glob = List("**/*.py")
48:     exclude = List("third_party/**")
49:     check = "uv run ty check --error-on-warning .mise"
50:   }
51: }
52: 
53: hooks {
54:   ["pre-commit"] {
55:     fix = true
56:     stash = "git"
57:     steps {
58:       ...lintSteps
59:     }
60:   }
61:   ["check"] {
62:     steps {
63:       ...lintSteps
64:     }
65:   }
66:   ["fix"] {
67:     fix = true
68:     steps {
69:       ...lintSteps
70:     }
71:   }
72: }

(End of file - total 72 lines)
</content>

name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

permissions:

concurrency:

display.text:
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

permissions:
  contents: read

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}

jobs:
  hygiene:
    name: hygiene
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 30
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps
      - run: mise run ci:lint

  docs:
    name: docs
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 60
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps
      - run: mise run //docs:build

  variants:
    name: variant / ${{ matrix.mise_env }}
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}
    timeout-minutes: 120
    env:
      MISE_ENV: ${{ matrix.mise_env }}
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        include:
          - mise_env: linux-arm64-egl
            runner: ubuntu-24.04-arm
          - mise_env: linux-arm64-vulkan
            runner: ubuntu-24.04-arm
          - mise_env: linux-x64-egl
            runner: ubuntu-latest
          - mise_env: linux-x64-vulkan
            runner: ubuntu-latest
          - mise_env: macos-arm64-metal
            runner: macos-latest
          - mise_env: macos-arm64-vulkan
            runner: macos-latest
          - mise_env: macos-arm64-egl
            runner: macos-latest
          - mise_env: ios-arm64-metal
            runner: macos-latest
          - mise_env: ios-simulator-arm64-metal
            runner: macos-latest
          - mise_env: windows-x64-vulkan
            runner: windows-2022
          - mise_env: windows-x64-wgl
            runner: windows-2022
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps

      - run: mise run configure
      - name: Build native library
        if: ${{ startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run build
      - name: Test native library
        if: ${{ !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run test

      - name: Build Java FFM binding
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run //bindings/java-ffm:build
      - name: Build Java JNI binding
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run //bindings/java-jni:build
      - name: Test .NET binding
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run //bindings/dotnet:test
      # linux-arm64: no Kotlin/Native host — https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-36871
      # windows: no MSVC target — https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/SKIKO-446
      - name: Test Kotlin/Native binding
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'windows-') && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'linux-arm64-') }}
        run: mise run //bindings/kotlin-native:build
      - name: Test Rust binding
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run //bindings/rust:ci
      - name: Test Swift binding
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'macos-') }}
        run: mise run //bindings/swift:test
      - name: Test Zig binding
        if: ${{ !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run //bindings/zig:ci

      - name: Build LWJGL example
        if: ${{ contains(matrix.mise_env, 'vulkan') }}
        run: mise run //examples/lwjgl-map:build
      - name: Test Rust example
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run //examples/rust-map:ci
      - name: Build Swift example
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env == 'macos-arm64-metal' }}
        run: mise run //examples/swift-map:build
      - name: Build Zig example
        if: ${{ matrix.mise_env != 'macos-arm64-egl' && !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run //examples/zig-map:build
      - name: Run Zig readback example
        if: ${{ !startsWith(matrix.mise_env, 'ios-') }}
        run: mise run //examples/zig-readback:run

  required:
    name: ci-required
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 5
    if: ${{ always() }}
    needs:
      - hygiene
      - docs
      - variants
    steps:
      - name: Fail if required jobs failed
        if: ${{ contains(needs.*.result, 'failure') || contains(needs.*.result, 'cancelled') || contains(needs.*.result, 'skipped') }}
        run: |
          echo '${{ toJSON(needs) }}'
          exit 1

      - run: true

1: [tasks.build]
2: depends = ["//:ensure-native-library"]
3: dir = "{{env.MLN_FFI_REPO_ROOT}}"
4: run = "cargo build -p maplibre-native-sys -p maplibre-native-core -p maplibre-native"

part.text:
**Planning plural manifest-driven CI design**

**Designing single declarative manifest approach**

The drift is broader than the matrix: `ci.yml` also embeds which bindings and examples support each variant through repeated expressions. I’m going to model both variant runners and per-component support in declarative TOML, then generate the full workflow so no hand-edited compatibility policy remains hidden in YAML; setup, ordering, job names, and commands will stay unchanged.

state.output:
<path>/app/.mise/config.linux-arm64-egl.toml</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: [env]
2: MLN_FFI_VARIANT = "linux-arm64-egl"
3: MLN_FFI_TARGET_TRIPLE = "linux-arm64"
4: MLN_FFI_RENDER_BACKEND = "opengl"
5: MLN_FFI_OPENGL_CONTEXT_PROVIDER = "egl"

part.text: The implementation will use two small source manifests under `.github/ci/`: one for variants/runners/native mode and one for the ordered CI steps and their supported variants. A standard-library Python generator will validate those manifests against every `.mise/config.*.toml`, render `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, expose `mise run ci:generate`/`ci:check`, and participate in `hk check`/`hk fix`; focused unit tests will cover rendering and invalid policy.

*** Add File: /app/scripts/generate_ci.py
+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Generate the checked-in CI workflow from target support manifests."""
+
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import argparse
+import difflib
+import re
+import sys
+import tomllib
+from dataclasses import dataclass
+from pathlib import Path
+
+ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
+TARGETS_MANIFEST = Path(".github/ci/targets.toml")
+STEPS_MANIFEST = Path(".github/ci/steps.toml")
+WORKFLOW = Path(".github/workflows/ci.yml")
+ID_PATTERN = re.compile(r"^[a-z0-9]+(?:-[a-z0-9]+)*$")
+
+
+class PolicyError(ValueError):
+    """The CI manifests do not describe a valid support policy."""
+
+
+@dataclass(frozen=True)
+class Target:
+    id: str
+    runner: str
+
+
+@dataclass(frozen=True)
+class Step:
+    id: str
+    name: str
+    run: str
+    targets: tuple[str, ...]
+    comment: str | None = None
+
+
+@dataclass(frozen=True)
+class Policy:
+    targets: tuple[Target, ...]
+    steps: tuple[Step, ...]
+
+
+def _load_toml(path: Path) -> dict[str, object]:
+    with path.open("rb") as source:
+        return tomllib.load(source)
+
+
+def _required_string(record: dict[str, object], key: str, context: str) -> str:
+    value = record.get(key)
+    if not isinstance(value, str) or not value:
+        raise PolicyError(f"{context}.{key} must be a non-empty string")
+    return value
+
+
+def _records(document: dict[str, object], key: str, path: Path) -> list[dict[str, object]]:
+    value = document.get(key)
+    if not isinstance(value, list) or not value:
+        raise PolicyError(f"{path}: {key} must be a non-empty array of tables")
+    if not all(isinstance(record, dict) for record in value):
+        raise PolicyError(f"{path}: every {key} entry must be a table")
+    return value  # type: ignore[return-value]
+
+
+def load_policy(root: Path = ROOT) -> Policy:
+    target_path = root / TARGETS_MANIFEST
+    step_path = root / STEPS_MANIFEST
+    target_records = _records(_load_toml(target_path), "targets", target_path)
+    step_records = _records(_load_toml(step_path), "steps", step_path)
+
+    targets: list[Target] = []
+    target_ids: set[str] = set()
+    for index, record in enumerate(target_records):
+        context = f"{target_path}: targets[{index}]"
+        target_id = _required_string(record, "id", context)
+        if target_id in target_ids:
+            raise PolicyError(f"{context}.id duplicates {target_id!r}")
+        target_ids.add(target_id)
+        targets.append(Target(target_id, _required_string(record, "runner", context)))
+
+    config_ids: set[str] = set()
+    for config_path in sorted((root / ".mise").glob("config.*.toml")):
+        document = _load_toml(config_path)
+        env = document.get("env")
+        if not isinstance(env, dict):
+            raise PolicyError(f"{config_path}: env must be a table")
+        variant = env.get("MLN_FFI_VARIANT")
+        if not isinstance(variant, str):
+            raise PolicyError(f"{config_path}: env.MLN_FFI_VARIANT must be a string")
+        expected = config_path.name.removeprefix("config.").removesuffix(".toml")
+        if variant != expected:
+            raise PolicyError(
+                f"{config_path}: MLN_FFI_VARIANT is {variant!r}, expected {expected!r}"
+            )
+        config_ids.add(variant)
+
+    missing_targets = sorted(config_ids - target_ids)
+    missing_configs = sorted(target_ids - config_ids)
+    if missing_targets or missing_configs:
+        details = []
+        if missing_targets:
+            details.append(f"missing CI targets: {', '.join(missing_targets)}")
+        if missing_configs:
+            details.append(f"missing mise configs: {', '.join(missing_configs)}")
+        raise PolicyError("; ".join(details))
+
+    steps: list[Step] = []
+    step_ids: set[str] = set()
+    covered_targets: set[str] = set()
+    for index, record in enumerate(step_records):
+        context = f"{step_path}: steps[{index}]"
+        step_id = _required_string(record, "id", context)
+        if not ID_PATTERN.fullmatch(step_id):
+            raise PolicyError(f"{context}.id must match {ID_PATTERN.pattern!r}")
+        if step_id in step_ids:
+            raise PolicyError(f"{context}.id duplicates {step_id!r}")
+        step_ids.add(step_id)
+
+        supported = record.get("targets")
+        if not isinstance(supported, list) or not supported:
+            raise PolicyError(f"{context}.targets must be a non-empty string array")
+        if not all(isinstance(target, str) for target in supported):
+            raise PolicyError(f"{context}.targets must contain only strings")
+        if len(supported) != len(set(supported)):
+            raise PolicyError(f"{context}.targets contains duplicates")
+        unknown = sorted(set(supported) - target_ids)
+        if unknown:
+            raise PolicyError(f"{context}.targets contains unknown targets: {', '.join(unknown)}")
+
+        comment = record.get("comment")
+        if comment is not None and (not isinstance(comment, str) or not comment):
+            raise PolicyError(f"{context}.comment must be a non-empty string")
+        covered_targets.update(supported)
+        steps.append(
+            Step(
+                step_id,
+                _required_string(record, "name", context),
+                _required_string(record, "run", context),
+                tuple(supported),
+                comment,
+            )
+        )
+
+    uncovered = sorted(target_ids - covered_targets)
+    if uncovered:
+        raise PolicyError(f"targets have no CI steps: {', '.join(uncovered)}")
+    return Policy(tuple(targets), tuple(steps))
+
+
+def render_workflow(policy: Policy) -> str:
+    lines = [
+        "# Generated by `mise run ci:generate`; edit .github/ci/*.toml instead.",
+        "name: CI",
+        "",
+        "on:",
+        "  push:",
+        "    branches: [main]",
+        "  pull_request:",
+        "    branches: [main]",
+        "",
+        "permissions:",
+        "  contents: read",
+        "",
+        "concurrency:",
+        "  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref }}",
+        "  cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}",
+        "",
+        "jobs:",
+        "  hygiene:",
+        "    name: hygiene",
+        "    runs-on: ubuntu-latest",
+        "    timeout-minutes: 30",
+        "    steps:",
+        "      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3",
+        "      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps",
+        "      - run: mise run ci:lint",
+        "",
+        "  docs:",
+        "    name: docs",
+        "    runs-on: ubuntu-latest",
+        "    timeout-minutes: 60",
+        "    steps:",
+        "      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3",
+        "      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps",
+        "      - run: mise run //docs:build",
+        "",
+        "  variants:",
+        "    name: variant / ${{ matrix.mise_env }}",
+        "    runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}",
+        "    timeout-minutes: 120",
+        "    env:",
+        "      MISE_ENV: ${{ matrix.mise_env }}",
+        "    strategy:",
+        "      fail-fast: false",
+        "      matrix:",
+        "        include:",
+    ]
+    for target in policy.targets:
+        supported_steps = [step.id for step in policy.steps if target.id in step.targets]
+        lines.extend(
+            [
+                f"          - mise_env: {target.id}",
+                f"            runner: {target.runner}",
+                f"            ci_steps: [{', '.join(supported_steps)}]",
+            ]
+        )
+
+    lines.extend(
+        [
+            "    steps:",
+            "      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3",
+            "      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps",
+            "",
+            "      - run: mise run configure",
+        ]
+    )
+    for step in policy.steps:
+        if step.comment:
+            lines.append(f"      # {step.comment}")
+        lines.extend(
+            [
+                f"      - name: {step.name}",
+                f"        if: ${{{{ contains(matrix.ci_steps, '{step.id}') }}}}",
+                f"        run: {step.run}",
+            ]
+        )
+
+    lines.extend(
+        [
+            "",
+            "  required:",
+            "    name: ci-required",
+            "    runs-on: ubuntu-latest",
+            "    timeout-minutes: 5",
+            "    if: ${{ always() }}",
+            "    needs:",
+            "      - hygiene",
+            "      - docs",
+            "      - variants",
+            "    steps:",
+            "      - name: Fail if required jobs failed",
+            "        if: ${{ contains(needs.*.result, 'failure') || contains(needs.*.result, 'cancelled') || contains(needs.*.result, 'skipped') }}",
+            "        run: |",
+            "          echo '${{ toJSON(needs) }}'",
+            "          exit 1",
+            "",
+            "      - run: true",
+            "",
+        ]
+    )
+    return "\n".join(lines)
+
+
+def check_workflow(path: Path, generated: str) -> bool:
+    existing = path.read_text() if path.exists() else ""
+    if existing == generated:
+        return True
+    sys.stderr.writelines(
+        difflib.unified_diff(
+            existing.splitlines(keepends=True),
+            generated.splitlines(keepends=True),
+            fromfile=str(path),
+            tofile=f"{path} (generated)",
+        )
+    )
+    sys.stderr.write("CI workflow is stale; run `mise run ci:generate`.\n")
+    return False
+
+
+def main() -> int:
+    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
+    parser.add_argument("--check", action="store_true", help="fail if the workflow is stale")
+    args = parser.parse_args()
+
+    try:
+        generated = render_workflow(load_policy())
+    except (OSError, tomllib.TOMLDecodeError, PolicyError) as error:
+        parser.error(str(error))
+
+    workflow_path = ROOT / WORKFLOW
+    if args.check:
+        return 0 if check_workflow(workflow_path, generated) else 1
+    workflow_path.write_text(generated)
+    return 0
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    raise SystemExit(main())

--- /app/scripts/generate_ci.py
+++ /app/scripts/generate_ci.py
@@ -0,0 +1,290 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Generate the checked-in CI workflow from target support manifests."""
+
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import argparse
+import difflib
+import re
+import sys
+import tomllib
+from dataclasses import dataclass
+from pathlib import Path
+
+ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
+TARGETS_MANIFEST = Path(".github/ci/targets.toml")
+STEPS_MANIFEST = Path(".github/ci/steps.toml")
+WORKFLOW = Path(".github/workflows/ci.yml")
+ID_PATTERN = re.compile(r"^[a-z0-9]+(?:-[a-z0-9]+)*$")
+
+
+class PolicyError(ValueError):
+    """The CI manifests do not describe a valid support policy."""
+
+
+@dataclass(frozen=True)
+class Target:
+    id: str
+    runner: str
+
+
+@dataclass(frozen=True)
+class Step:
+    id: str
+    name: str
+    run: str
+    targets: tuple[str, ...]
+    comment: str | None = None
+
+
+@dataclass(frozen=True)
+class Policy:
+    targets: tuple[Target, ...]
+    steps: tuple[Step, ...]
+
+
+def _load_toml(path: Path) -> dict[str, object]:
+    with path.open("rb") as source:
+        return tomllib.load(source)
+
+
+def _required_string(record: dict[str, object], key: str, context: str) -> str:
+    value = record.get(key)
+    if not isinstance(value, str) or not value:
+        raise PolicyError(f"{context}.{key} must be a non-empty string")
+    return value
+
+
+def _records(document: dict[str, object], key: str, path: Path) -> list[dict[str, object]]:
+    value = document.get(key)
+    if not isinstance(value, list) or not value:
+        raise PolicyError(f"{path}: {key} must be a non-empty array of tables")
+    if not all(isinstance(record, dict) for record in value):
+        raise PolicyError(f"{path}: every {key} entry must be a table")
+    return value  # type: ignore[return-value]
+
+
+def load_policy(root: Path = ROOT) -> Policy:
+    target_path = root / TARGETS_MANIFEST
+    step_path = root / STEPS_MANIFEST
+    target_records = _records(_load_toml(target_path), "targets", target_path)
+    step_records = _records(_load_toml(step_path), "steps", step_path)
+
+    targets: list[Target] = []
+    target_ids: set[str] = set()
+    for index, record in enumerate(target_records):
+        context = f"{target_path}: targets[{index}]"
+        target_id = _required_string(record, "id", context)
+        if target_id in target_ids:
+            raise PolicyError(f"{context}.id duplicates {target_id!r}")
+        target_ids.add(target_id)
+        targets.append(Target(target_id, _required_string(record, "runner", context)))
+
+    config_ids: set[str] = set()
+    for config_path in sorted((root / ".mise").glob("config.*.toml")):
+        document = _load_toml(config_path)
+        env = document.get("env")
+        if not isinstance(env, dict):
+            raise PolicyError(f"{config_path}: env must be a table")
+        variant = env.get("MLN_FFI_VARIANT")
+        if not isinstance(variant, str):
+            raise PolicyError(f"{config_path}: env.MLN_FFI_VARIANT must be a string")
+        expected = config_path.name.removeprefix("config.").removesuffix(".toml")
+        if variant != expected:
+            raise PolicyError(
+                f"{config_path}: MLN_FFI_VARIANT is {variant!r}, expected {expected!r}"
+            )
+        config_ids.add(variant)
+
+    missing_targets = sorted(config_ids - target_ids)
+    missing_configs = sorted(target_ids - config_ids)
+    if missing_targets or missing_configs:
+        details = []
+        if missing_targets:
+            details.append(f"missing CI targets: {', '.join(missing_targets)}")
+        if missing_configs:
+            details.append(f"missing mise configs: {', '.join(missing_configs)}")
+        raise PolicyError("; ".join(details))
+
+    steps: list[Step] = []
+    step_ids: set[str] = set()
+    covered_targets: set[str] = set()
+    for index, record in enumerate(step_records):
+        context = f"{step_path}: steps[{index}]"
+        step_id = _required_string(record, "id", context)
+        if not ID_PATTERN.fullmatch(step_id):
+            raise PolicyError(f"{context}.id must match {ID_PATTERN.pattern!r}")
+        if step_id in step_ids:
+            raise PolicyError(f"{context}.id duplicates {step_id!r}")
+        step_ids.add(step_id)
+
+        supported = record.get("targets")
+        if not isinstance(supported, list) or not supported:
+            raise PolicyError(f"{context}.targets must be a non-empty string array")
+        if not all(isinstance(target, str) for target in supported):
+            raise PolicyError(f"{context}.targets must contain only strings")
+        if len(supported) != len(set(supported)):
+            raise PolicyError(f"{context}.targets contains duplicates")
+        unknown = sorted(set(supported) - target_ids)
+        if unknown:
+            raise PolicyError(f"{context}.targets contains unknown targets: {', '.join(unknown)}")
+
+        comment = record.get("comment")
+        if comment is not None and (not isinstance(comment, str) or not comment):
+            raise PolicyError(f"{context}.comment must be a non-empty string")
+        covered_targets.update(supported)
+        steps.append(
+            Step(
+                step_id,
+                _required_string(record, "name", context),
+                _required_string(record, "run", context),
+                tuple(supported),
+                comment,
+            )
+        )
+
+    uncovered = sorted(target_ids - covered_targets)
+    if uncovered:
+        raise PolicyError(f"targets have no CI steps: {', '.join(uncovered)}")
+    return Policy(tuple(targets), tuple(steps))
+
+
+def render_workflow(policy: Policy) -> str:
+    lines = [
+        "# Generated by `mise run ci:generate`; edit .github/ci/*.toml instead.",
+        "name: CI",
+        "",
+        "on:",
+        "  push:",
+        "    branches: [main]",
+        "  pull_request:",
+        "    branches: [main]",
+        "",
+        "permissions:",
+        "  contents: read",
+        "",
+        "concurrency:",
+        "  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref }}",
+        "  cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}",
+        "",
+        "jobs:",
+        "  hygiene:",
+        "    name: hygiene",
+        "    runs-on: ubuntu-latest",
+        "    timeout-minutes: 30",
+        "    steps:",
+        "      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3",
+        "      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps",
+        "      - run: mise run ci:lint",
+        "",
+        "  docs:",
+        "    name: docs",
+        "    runs-on: ubuntu-latest",
+        "    timeout-minutes: 60",
+        "    steps:",
+        "      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3",
+        "      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps",
+        "      - run: mise run //docs:build",
+        "",
+        "  variants:",
+        "    name: variant / ${{ matrix.mise_env }}",
+        "    runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}",
+        "    timeout-minutes: 120",
+        "    env:",
+        "      MISE_ENV: ${{ matrix.mise_env }}",
+        "    strategy:",
+        "      fail-fast: false",
+        "      matrix:",
+        "        include:",
+    ]
+    for target in policy.targets:
+        supported_steps = [step.id for step in policy.steps if target.id in step.targets]
+        lines.extend(
+            [
+                f"          - mise_env: {target.id}",
+                f"            runner: {target.runner}",
+                f"            ci_steps: [{', '.join(supported_steps)}]",
+            ]
+        )
+
+    lines.extend(
+        [
+            "    steps:",
+            "      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3",
+            "      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps",
+            "",
+            "      - run: mise run configure",
+        ]
+    )
+    for step in policy.steps:
+        if step.comment:
+            lines.append(f"      # {step.comment}")
+        lines.extend(
+            [
+                f"      - name: {step.name}",
+                f"        if: ${{{{ contains(matrix.ci_steps, '{step.id}') }}}}",
+                f"        run: {step.run}",
+            ]
+        )
+
+    lines.extend(
+        [
+            "",
+            "  required:",
+            "    name: ci-required",
+            "    runs-on: ubuntu-latest",
+            "    timeout-minutes: 5",
+            "    if: ${{ always() }}",
+            "    needs:",
+            "      - hygiene",
+            "      - docs",
+            "      - variants",
+            "    steps:",
+            "      - name: Fail if required jobs failed",
+            "        if: ${{ contains(needs.*.result, 'failure') || contains(needs.*.result, 'cancelled') || contains(needs.*.result, 'skipped') }}",
+            "        run: |",
+            "          echo '${{ toJSON(needs) }}'",
+            "          exit 1",
+            "",
+            "      - run: true",
+            "",
+        ]
+    )
+    return "\n".join(lines)
+
+
+def check_workflow(path: Path, generated: str) -> bool:
+    existing = path.read_text() if path.exists() else ""
+    if existing == generated:
+        return True
+    sys.stderr.writelines(
+        difflib.unified_diff(
+            existing.splitlines(keepends=True),
+            generated.splitlines(keepends=True),
+            fromfile=str(path),
+            tofile=f"{path} (generated)",
+        )
+    )
+    sys.stderr.write("CI workflow is stale; run `mise run ci:generate`.\n")
+    return False
+
+
+def main() -> int:
+    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
+    parser.add_argument("--check", action="store_true", help="fail if the workflow is stale")
+    args = parser.parse_args()
+
+    try:
+        generated = render_workflow(load_policy())
+    except (OSError, tomllib.TOMLDecodeError, PolicyError) as error:
+        parser.error(str(error))
+
+    workflow_path = ROOT / WORKFLOW
+    if args.check:
+        return 0 if check_workflow(workflow_path, generated) else 1
+    workflow_path.write_text(generated)
+    return 0
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    raise SystemExit(main())

@@ -0,0 +1,68 @@
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import importlib.util
+import tempfile
+import unittest
+from pathlib import Path
+
+SCRIPT = Path(__file__).with_name("generate_ci.py")
+SPEC = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location("generate_ci", SCRIPT)
+assert SPEC and SPEC.loader
+generate_ci = importlib.util.module_from_spec(SPEC)
+SPEC.loader.exec_module(generate_ci)
+
+
+class GenerateCiTest(unittest.TestCase):
+    def test_checked_in_workflow_matches_manifests(self) -> None:
+        policy = generate_ci.load_policy()
+        generated = generate_ci.render_workflow(policy)
+
+        self.assertEqual((generate_ci.ROOT / generate_ci.WORKFLOW).read_text(), generated)
+        self.assertIn("mise_env: ios-arm64-metal", generated)
+        self.assertIn("contains(matrix.ci_steps, 'native-build')", generated)
+        self.assertIn("contains(matrix.ci_steps, 'swift-example')", generated)
+
+    def test_unknown_step_target_is_rejected(self) -> None:
+        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as directory:
+            root = Path(directory)
+            (root / ".github/ci").mkdir(parents=True)
+            (root / ".mise").mkdir()
+            (root / ".github/ci/targets.toml").write_text(
+                '[[targets]]\nid = "linux-x64-vulkan"\nrunner = "ubuntu-latest"\n'
+            )
+            (root / ".github/ci/steps.toml").write_text(
+                '[[steps]]\nid = "native-test"\nname = "Test native"\n'
+                'run = "mise run test"\ntargets = ["unknown"]\n'
+            )
+            (root / ".mise/config.linux-x64-vulkan.toml").write_text(
+                '[env]\nMLN_FFI_VARIANT = "linux-x64-vulkan"\n'
+            )
+
+            with self.assertRaisesRegex(generate_ci.PolicyError, "unknown targets: unknown"):
+                generate_ci.load_policy(root)
+
+    def test_mise_variant_without_ci_target_is_rejected(self) -> None:
+        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as directory:
+            root = Path(directory)
+            (root / ".github/ci").mkdir(parents=True)
+            (root / ".mise").mkdir()
+            (root / ".github/ci/targets.toml").write_text(
+                '[[targets]]\nid = "linux-x64-vulkan"\nrunner = "ubuntu-latest"\n'
+            )
+            (root / ".github/ci/steps.toml").write_text(
+                '[[steps]]\nid = "native-test"\nname = "Test native"\n'
+                'run = "mise run test"\ntargets = ["linux-x64-vulkan"]\n'
+            )
+            for variant in ("linux-x64-vulkan", "linux-x64-egl"):
+                (root / f".mise/config.{variant}.toml").write_text(
+                    f'[env]\nMLN_FFI_VARIANT = "{variant}"\n'
+                )
+
+            with self.assertRaisesRegex(
+                generate_ci.PolicyError, "missing CI targets: linux-x64-egl"
+            ):
+                generate_ci.load_policy(root)
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    unittest.main()

Index: /app/scripts/generate_ci.py
===================================================================
--- /app/scripts/generate_ci.py
+++ /app/scripts/generate_ci.py
@@ -0,0 +1,290 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Generate the checked-in CI workflow from target support manifests."""
+
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import argparse
+import difflib
+import re
+import sys
+import tomllib
+from dataclasses import dataclass
+from pathlib import Path
+
+ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
+TARGETS_MANIFEST = Path(".github/ci/targets.toml")
+STEPS_MANIFEST = Path(".github/ci/steps.toml")
+WORKFLOW = Path(".github/workflows/ci.yml")
+ID_PATTERN = re.compile(r"^[a-z0-9]+(?:-[a-z0-9]+)*$")
+
+
+class PolicyError(ValueError):
+    """The CI manifests do not describe a valid support policy."""
+
+
+@dataclass(frozen=True)
+class Target:
+    id: str
+    runner: str
+
+
+@dataclass(frozen=True)
+class Step:
+    id: str
+    name: str
+    run: str
+    targets: tuple[str, ...]
+    comment: str | None = None
+
+
+@dataclass(frozen=True)
+class Policy:
+    targets: tuple[Target, ...]
+    steps: tuple[Step, ...]
+
+
+def _load_toml(path: Path) -> dict[str, object]:
+    with path.open("rb") as source:
+        return tomllib.load(source)
+
+
+def _required_string(record: dict[str, object], key: str, context: str) -> str:
+    value = record.get(key)
+    if not isinstance(value, str) or not value:
+        raise PolicyError(f"{context}.{key} must be a non-empty string")
+    return value
+
+
+def _records(document: dict[str, object], key: str, path: Path) -> list[dict[str, object]]:
+    value = document.get(key)
+    if not isinstance(value, list) or not value:
+        raise PolicyError(f"{path}: {key} must be a non-empty array of tables")
+    if not all(isinstance(record, dict) for record in value):
+        raise PolicyError(f"{path}: every {key} entry must be a table")
+    return value  # type: ignore[return-value]
+
+
+def load_policy(root: Path = ROOT) -> Policy:
+    target_path = root / TARGETS_MANIFEST
+    step_path = root / STEPS_MANIFEST
+    target_records = _records(_load_toml(target_path), "targets", target_path)
+    step_records = _records(_load_toml(step_path), "steps", step_path)
+
+    targets: list[Target] = []
+    target_ids: set[str] = set()
+    for index, record in enumerate(target_records):
+        context = f"{target_path}: targets[{index}]"
+        target_id = _required_string(record, "id", context)
+        if target_id in target_ids:
+            raise PolicyError(f"{context}.id duplicates {target_id!r}")
+        target_ids.add(target_id)
+        targets.append(Target(target_id, _required_string(record, "runner", context)))
+
+    config_ids: set[str] = set()
+    for config_path in sorted((root / ".mise").glob("config.*.toml")):
+        document = _load_toml(config_path)
+        env = document.get("env")
+        if not isinstance(env, dict):
+            raise PolicyError(f"{config_path}: env must be a table")
+        variant = env.get("MLN_FFI_VARIANT")
+        if not isinstance(variant, str):
+            raise PolicyError(f"{config_path}: env.MLN_FFI_VARIANT must be a string")
+        expected = config_path.name.removeprefix("config.").removesuffix(".toml")
+        if variant != expected:
+            raise PolicyError(
+                f"{config_path}: MLN_FFI_VARIANT is {variant!r}, expected {expected!r}"
+            )
+        config_ids.add(variant)
+
+    missing_targets = sorted(config_ids - target_ids)
+    missing_configs = sorted(target_ids - config_ids)
+    if missing_targets or missing_configs:
+        details = []
+        if missing_targets:
+            details.append(f"missing CI targets: {', '.join(missing_targets)}")
+        if missing_configs:
+            details.append(f"missing mise configs: {', '.join(missing_configs)}")
+        raise PolicyError("; ".join(details))
+
+    steps: list[Step] = []
+    step_ids: set[str] = set()
+    covered_targets: set[str] = set()
+    for index, record in enumerate(step_records):
+        context = f"{step_path}: steps[{index}]"
+        step_id = _required_string(record, "id", context)
+        if not ID_PATTERN.fullmatch(step_id):
+            raise PolicyError(f"{context}.id must match {ID_PATTERN.pattern!r}")
+        if step_id in step_ids:
+            raise PolicyError(f"{context}.id duplicates {step_id!r}")
+        step_ids.add(step_id)
+
+        supported = record.get("targets")
+        if not isinstance(supported, list) or not supported:
+            raise PolicyError(f"{context}.targets must be a non-empty string array")
+        if not all(isinstance(target, str) for target in supported):
+            raise PolicyError(f"{context}.targets must contain only strings")
+        if len(supported) != len(set(supported)):
+            raise PolicyError(f"{context}.targets contains duplicates")
+        unknown = sorted(set(supported) - target_ids)
+        if unknown:
+            raise PolicyError(f"{context}.targets contains unknown targets: {', '.join(unknown)}")
+
+        comment = record.get("comment")
+        if comment is not None and (not isinstance(comment, str) or not comment):
+            raise PolicyError(f"{context}.comment must be a non-empty string")
+        covered_targets.update(supported)
+        steps.append(
+            Step(
+                step_id,
+                _required_string(record, "name", context),
+                _required_string(record, "run", context),
+                tuple(supported),
+                comment,
+            )
+        )
+
+    uncovered = sorted(target_ids - covered_targets)
+    if uncovered:
+        raise PolicyError(f"targets have no CI steps: {', '.join(uncovered)}")
+    return Policy(tuple(targets), tuple(steps))
+
+
+def render_workflow(policy: Policy) -> str:
+    lines = [
+        "# Generated by `mise run ci:generate`; edit .github/ci/*.toml instead.",
+        "name: CI",
+        "",
+        "on:",
+        "  push:",
+        "    branches: [main]",
+        "  pull_request:",
+        "    branches: [main]",
+        "",
+        "permissions:",
+        "  contents: read",
+        "",
+        "concurrency:",
+        "  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref }}",
+        "  cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}",
+        "",
+        "jobs:",
+        "  hygiene:",
+        "    name: hygiene",
+        "    runs-on: ubuntu-latest",
+        "    timeout-minutes: 30",
+        "    steps:",
+        "      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3",
+        "      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps",
+        "      - run: mise run ci:lint",
+        "",
+        "  docs:",
+        "    name: docs",
+        "    runs-on: ubuntu-latest",
+        "    timeout-minutes: 60",
+        "    steps:",
+        "      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3",
+        "      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps",
+        "      - run: mise run //docs:build",
+        "",
+        "  variants:",
+        "    name: variant / ${{ matrix.mise_env }}",
+        "    runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}",
+        "    timeout-minutes: 120",
+        "    env:",
+        "      MISE_ENV: ${{ matrix.mise_env }}",
+        "    strategy:",
+        "      fail-fast: false",
+        "      matrix:",
+        "        include:",
+    ]
+    for target in policy.targets:
+        supported_steps = [step.id for step in policy.steps if target.id in step.targets]
+        lines.extend(
+            [
+                f"          - mise_env: {target.id}",
+                f"            runner: {target.runner}",
+                f"            ci_steps: [{', '.join(supported_steps)}]",
+            ]
+        )
+
+    lines.extend(
+        [
+            "    steps:",
+            "      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3",
+            "      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps",
+            "",
+            "      - run: mise run configure",
+        ]
+    )
+    for step in policy.steps:
+        if step.comment:
+            lines.append(f"      # {step.comment}")
+        lines.extend(
+            [
+                f"      - name: {step.name}",
+                f"        if: ${{{{ contains(matrix.ci_steps, '{step.id}') }}}}",
+                f"        run: {step.run}",
+            ]
+        )
+
+    lines.extend(
+        [
+            "",
+            "  required:",
+            "    name: ci-required",
+            "    runs-on: ubuntu-latest",
+            "    timeout-minutes: 5",
+            "    if: ${{ always() }}",
+            "    needs:",
+            "      - hygiene",
+            "      - docs",
+            "      - variants",
+            "    steps:",
+            "      - name: Fail if required jobs failed",
+            "        if: ${{ contains(needs.*.result, 'failure') || contains(needs.*.result, 'cancelled') || contains(needs.*.result, 'skipped') }}",
+            "        run: |",
+            "          echo '${{ toJSON(needs) }}'",
+            "          exit 1",
+            "",
+            "      - run: true",
+            "",
+        ]
+    )
+    return "\n".join(lines)
+
+
+def check_workflow(path: Path, generated: str) -> bool:
+    existing = path.read_text() if path.exists() else ""
+    if existing == generated:
+        return True
+    sys.stderr.writelines(
+        difflib.unified_diff(
+            existing.splitlines(keepends=True),
+            generated.splitlines(keepends=True),
+            fromfile=str(path),
+            tofile=f"{path} (generated)",
+        )
+    )
+    sys.stderr.write("CI workflow is stale; run `mise run ci:generate`.\n")
+    return False
+
+
+def main() -> int:
+    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
+    parser.add_argument("--check", action="store_true", help="fail if the workflow is stale")
+    args = parser.parse_args()
+
+    try:
+        generated = render_workflow(load_policy())
+    except (OSError, tomllib.TOMLDecodeError, PolicyError) as error:
+        parser.error(str(error))
+
+    workflow_path = ROOT / WORKFLOW
+    if args.check:
+        return 0 if check_workflow(workflow_path, generated) else 1
+    workflow_path.write_text(generated)
+    return 0
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    raise SystemExit(main())

--- /app/scripts/test_generate_ci.py
+++ /app/scripts/test_generate_ci.py
@@ -0,0 +1,68 @@
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import importlib.util
+import tempfile
+import unittest
+from pathlib import Path
+
+SCRIPT = Path(__file__).with_name("generate_ci.py")
+SPEC = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location("generate_ci", SCRIPT)
+assert SPEC and SPEC.loader
+generate_ci = importlib.util.module_from_spec(SPEC)
+SPEC.loader.exec_module(generate_ci)
+
+
+class GenerateCiTest(unittest.TestCase):
+    def test_checked_in_workflow_matches_manifests(self) -> None:
+        policy = generate_ci.load_policy()
+        generated = generate_ci.render_workflow(policy)
+
+        self.assertEqual((generate_ci.ROOT / generate_ci.WORKFLOW).read_text(), generated)
+        self.assertIn("mise_env: ios-arm64-metal", generated)
+        self.assertIn("contains(matrix.ci_steps, 'native-build')", generated)
+        self.assertIn("contains(matrix.ci_steps, 'swift-example')", generated)
+
+    def test_unknown_step_target_is_rejected(self) -> None:
+        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as directory:
+            root = Path(directory)
+            (root / ".github/ci").mkdir(parents=True)
+            (root / ".mise").mkdir()
+            (root / ".github/ci/targets.toml").write_text(
+                '[[targets]]\nid = "linux-x64-vulkan"\nrunner = "ubuntu-latest"\n'
+            )
+            (root / ".github/ci/steps.toml").write_text(
+                '[[steps]]\nid = "native-test"\nname = "Test native"\n'
+                'run = "mise run test"\ntargets = ["unknown"]\n'
+            )
+            (root / ".mise/config.linux-x64-vulkan.toml").write_text(
+                '[env]\nMLN_FFI_VARIANT = "linux-x64-vulkan"\n'
+            )
+
+            with self.assertRaisesRegex(generate_ci.PolicyError, "unknown targets: unknown"):
+                generate_ci.load_policy(root)
+
+    def test_mise_variant_without_ci_target_is_rejected(self) -> None:
+        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as directory:
+            root = Path(directory)
+            (root / ".github/ci").mkdir(parents=True)
+            (root / ".mise").mkdir()
+            (root / ".github/ci/targets.toml").write_text(
+                '[[targets]]\nid = "linux-x64-vulkan"\nrunner = "ubuntu-latest"\n'
+            )
+            (root / ".github/ci/steps.toml").write_text(
+                '[[steps]]\nid = "native-test"\nname = "Test native"\n'
+                'run = "mise run test"\ntargets = ["linux-x64-vulkan"]\n'
+            )
+            for variant in ("linux-x64-vulkan", "linux-x64-egl"):
+                (root / f".mise/config.{variant}.toml").write_text(
+                    f'[env]\nMLN_FFI_VARIANT = "{variant}"\n'
+                )
+
+            with self.assertRaisesRegex(
+                generate_ci.PolicyError, "missing CI targets: linux-x64-egl"
+            ):
+                generate_ci.load_policy(root)
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    unittest.main()

display.text:
# Generated by `mise run ci:generate`; edit .github/ci/*.toml instead.
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

permissions:
  contents: read

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}

jobs:
  hygiene:
    name: hygiene
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 30
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps
      - run: mise run ci:lint

  docs:
    name: docs
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 60
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps
      - run: mise run //docs:build

  variants:
    name: variant / ${{ matrix.mise_env }}
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}
    timeout-minutes: 120
    env:
      MISE_ENV: ${{ matrix.mise_env }}
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        include:
          - mise_env: linux-arm64-egl
            runner: ubuntu-24.04-arm
            ci_steps: [native-test, java-ffm, java-jni, dotnet, rust-binding, zig-binding, rust-example, zig-example, zig-readback]
          - mise_env: linux-arm64-vulkan
            runner: ubuntu-24.04-arm
            ci_steps: [native-test, java-ffm, java-jni, dotnet, rust-binding, zig-binding, lwjgl-example, rust-example, zig-example, zig-readback]
          - mise_env: linux-x64-egl
            runner: ubuntu-latest
            ci_steps: [native-test, java-ffm, java-jni, dotnet, kotlin-native, rust-binding, zig-binding, rust-example, zig-example, zig-readback]
          - mise_env: linux-x64-vulkan
            runner: ubuntu-latest
            ci_steps: [native-test, java-ffm, java-jni, dotnet, kotlin-native, rust-binding, zig-binding, lwjgl-example, rust-example, zig-example, zig-readback]
          - mise_env: macos-arm64-metal
            runner: macos-latest
            ci_steps: [native-test, java-ffm, java-jni, dotnet, kotlin-native, rust-binding, swift-binding, zig-binding, rust-example, swift-example, zig-example, zig-readback]
          - mise_env: macos-arm64-vulkan
            runner: macos-latest
            ci_steps: [native-test, java-ffm, java-jni, dotnet, kotlin-native, rust-binding, swift-binding, zig-binding, lwjgl-example, rust-example, zig-example, zig-readback]
          - mise_env: macos-arm64-egl
            runner: macos-latest
            ci_steps: [native-test, zig-binding, zig-readback]
          - mise_env: ios-arm64-metal
            runner: macos-latest
            ci_steps: [native-build]
          - mise_env: ios-simulator-arm64-metal
            runner: macos-latest
            ci_steps: [native-build]
          - mise_env: windows-x64-vulkan
            runner: windows-2022
            ci_steps: [native-test, java-ffm, java-jni, dotnet, rust-binding, zig-binding, lwjgl-example, rust-example, zig-example, zig-readback]
          - mise_env: windows-x64-wgl
            runner: windows-2022
            ci_steps: [native-test, java-ffm, java-jni, dotnet, rust-binding, zig-binding, rust-example, zig-example, zig-readback]
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps

      - run: mise run configure
      - name: Build native library
        if: ${{ contains(matrix.ci_steps, 'native-build') }}
        run: mise run build
      - name: Test native library
        if: ${{ contains(matrix.ci_steps, 'native-test') }}
        run: mise run test
      - name: Build Java FFM binding
        if: ${{ contains(matrix.ci_steps, 'java-ffm') }}
        run: mise run //bindings/java-ffm:build
      - name: Build Java JNI binding
        if: ${{ contains(matrix.ci_steps, 'java-jni') }}
        run: mise run //bindings/java-jni:build
      - name: Test .NET binding
        if: ${{ contains(matrix.ci_steps, 'dotnet') }}
        run: mise run //bindings/dotnet:test
      # linux-arm64 has no Kotlin/Native host (KT-36871); Windows has no MSVC target (SKIKO-446).
      - name: Test Kotlin/Native binding
        if: ${{ contains(matrix.ci_steps, 'kotlin-native') }}
        run: mise run //bindings/kotlin-native:build
      - name: Test Rust binding
        if: ${{ contains(matrix.ci_steps, 'rust-binding') }}
        run: mise run //bindings/rust:ci
      - name: Test Swift binding
        if: ${{ contains(matrix.ci_steps, 'swift-binding') }}
        run: mise run //bindings/swift:test
      - name: Test Zig binding
        if: ${{ contains(matrix.ci_steps, 'zig-binding') }}
        run: mise run //bindings/zig:ci
      - name: Build LWJGL example
        if: ${{ contains(matrix.ci_steps, 'lwjgl-example') }}
        run: mise run //examples/lwjgl-map:build
      - name: Test Rust example
        if: ${{ contains(matrix.ci_steps, 'rust-example') }}
        run: mise run //examples/rust-map:ci
      - name: Build Swift example
        if: ${{ contains(matrix.ci_steps, 'swift-example') }}
        run: mise run //examples/swift-map:build
      - name: Build Zig example
        if: ${{ contains(matrix.ci_steps, 'zig-example') }}
        run: mise run //examples/zig-map:build
      - name: Run Zig readback example
        if: ${{ contains(matrix.ci_steps, 'zig-readback') }}
        run: mise run //examples/zig-readback:run

  required:
    name: ci-required
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 5
    if: ${{ always() }}
    needs:
      - hygiene
      - docs
      - variants
    steps:
      - name: Fail if required jobs failed
        if: ${{ contains(needs.*.result, 'failure') || contains(needs.*.result, 'cancelled') || contains(needs.*.result, 'skipped') }}
        run: |
          echo '${{ toJSON(needs) }}'
          exit 1

      - run: true

part.text: The generated matrix now matches every old condition, including the exceptional macOS EGL and iOS cases. One enforcement nuance surfaced: `hk fix` regenerates but does not necessarily execute a step’s check command, so I’m adding an explicit `ci:check` to the hygiene task to guarantee generator tests run in CI, then formatting and re-running all focused checks.

state.output:
<path>/app/scripts/generate_ci.py</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: #!/usr/bin/env python3
2: """Generate the checked-in CI workflow from target support manifests."""
3: 
4: from __future__ import annotations
5: 
6: import argparse
7: import difflib
8: import re
9: import sys
10: import tomllib
11: from dataclasses import dataclass
12: from pathlib import Path
13: from typing import cast
14: 
15: ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
16: TARGETS_MANIFEST = Path(".github/ci/targets.toml")
17: STEPS_MANIFEST = Path(".github/ci/steps.toml")
18: WORKFLOW = Path(".github/workflows/ci.yml")
19: ID_PATTERN = re.compile(r"^[a-z0-9]+(?:-[a-z0-9]+)*$")
20: 
21: 
22: class PolicyError(ValueError):
23:     """The CI manifests do not describe a valid support policy."""
24: 
25: 
26: @dataclass(frozen=True)
27: class Target:
28:     id: str
29:     runner: str
30: 
31: 
32: @dataclass(frozen=True)
33: class Step:
34:     id: str
35:     name: str
36:     run: str
37:     targets: tuple[str, ...]
38:     comment: str | None = None
39: 
40: 
41: @dataclass(frozen=True)
42: class Policy:
43:     targets: tuple[Target, ...]
44:     steps: tuple[Step, ...]
45: 
46: 
47: def _load_toml(path: Path) -> dict[str, object]:
48:     with path.open("rb") as source:
49:         return tomllib.load(source)
50: 
51: 
52: def _required_string(record: dict[str, object], key: str, context: str) -> str:
53:     value = record.get(key)
54:     if not isinstance(value, str) or not value:
55:         raise PolicyError(f"{context}.{key} must be a non-empty string")
56:     return value
57: 
58: 
59: def _records(
60:     document: dict[str, object], key: str, path: Path
61: ) -> list[dict[str, object]]:
62:     value = document.get(key)
63:     if not isinstance(value, list) or not value:
64:         raise PolicyError(f"{path}: {key} must be a non-empty array of tables")
65:     if not all(isinstance(record, dict) for record in value):
66:         raise PolicyError(f"{path}: every {key} entry must be a table")
67:     return cast(list[dict[str, object]], value)
68: 
69: 
70: def load_policy(root: Path = ROOT) -> Policy:
71:     target_path = root / TARGETS_MANIFEST
72:     step_path = root / STEPS_MANIFEST
73:     target_records = _records(_load_toml(target_path), "targets", target_path)
74:     step_records = _records(_load_toml(step_path), "steps", step_path)
75: 
76:     targets: list[Target] = []
77:     target_ids: set[str] = set()
78:     for index, record in enumerate(target_records):
79:         context = f"{target_path}: targets[{index}]"
80:         target_id = _required_string(record, "id", context)
81:         if target_id in target_ids:
82:             raise PolicyError(f"{context}.id duplicates {target_id!r}")
83:         target_ids.add(target_id)
84:         targets.append(Target(target_id, _required_string(record, "runner", context)))
85: 
86:     config_ids: set[str] = set()
87:     for config_path in sorted((root / ".mise").glob("config.*.toml")):
88:         document = _load_toml(config_path)
89:         env = document.get("env")
90:         if not isinstance(env, dict):
91:             raise PolicyError(f"{config_path}: env must be a table")
92:         env = cast(dict[str, object], env)
93:         variant = env.get("MLN_FFI_VARIANT")
94:         if not isinstance(variant, str):
95:             raise PolicyError(f"{config_path}: env.MLN_FFI_VARIANT must be a string")
96:         expected = config_path.name.removeprefix("config.").removesuffix(".toml")
97:         if variant != expected:
98:             raise PolicyError(
99:                 f"{config_path}: MLN_FFI_VARIANT is {variant!r}, expected {expected!r}"
100:             )
101:         config_ids.add(variant)
102: 
103:     missing_targets = sorted(config_ids - target_ids)
104:     missing_configs = sorted(target_ids - config_ids)
105:     if missing_targets or missing_configs:
106:         details = []
107:         if missing_targets:
108:             details.append(f"missing CI targets: {', '.join(missing_targets)}")
109:         if missing_configs:
110:             details.append(f"missing mise configs: {', '.join(missing_configs)}")
111:         raise PolicyError("; ".join(details))
112: 
113:     steps: list[Step] = []
114:     step_ids: set[str] = set()
115:     covered_targets: set[str] = set()
116:     for index, record in enumerate(step_records):
117:         context = f"{step_path}: steps[{index}]"
118:         step_id = _required_string(record, "id", context)
119:         if not ID_PATTERN.fullmatch(step_id):
120:             raise PolicyError(f"{context}.id must match {ID_PATTERN.pattern!r}")
121:         if step_id in step_ids:
122:             raise PolicyError(f"{context}.id duplicates {step_id!r}")
123:         step_ids.add(step_id)
124: 
125:         supported = record.get("targets")
126:         if not isinstance(supported, list) or not supported:
127:             raise PolicyError(f"{context}.targets must be a non-empty string array")
128:         if not all(isinstance(target, str) for target in supported):
129:             raise PolicyError(f"{context}.targets must contain only strings")
130:         supported = cast(list[str], supported)
131:         if len(supported) != len(set(supported)):
132:             raise PolicyError(f"{context}.targets contains duplicates")
133:         unknown = sorted(set(supported) - target_ids)
134:         if unknown:
135:             raise PolicyError(

display.text:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Generate the checked-in CI workflow from target support manifests."""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import difflib
import re
import sys
import tomllib
from dataclasses import dataclass
from pathlib import Path
from typing import cast

ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
TARGETS_MANIFEST = Path(".github/ci/targets.toml")
STEPS_MANIFEST = Path(".github/ci/steps.toml")
WORKFLOW = Path(".github/workflows/ci.yml")
ID_PATTERN = re.compile(r"^[a-z0-9]+(?:-[a-z0-9]+)*$")


class PolicyError(ValueError):
    """The CI manifests do not describe a valid support policy."""


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class Target:
    id: str
    runner: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class Step:
    id: str
    name: str
    run: str
    targets: tuple[str, ...]
    comment: str | None = None


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class Policy:
    targets: tuple[Target, ...]
    steps: tuple[Step, ...]


def _load_toml(path: Path) -> dict[str, object]:
    with path.open("rb") as source:
        return tomllib.load(source)


def _required_string(record: dict[str, object], key: str, context: str) -> str:
    value = record.get(key)
    if not isinstance(value, str) or not value:
        raise PolicyError(f"{context}.{key} must be a non-empty string")
    return value


def _records(
    document: dict[str, object], key: str, path: Path
) -> list[dict[str, object]]:
    value = document.get(key)
    if not isinstance(value, list) or not value:
        raise PolicyError(f"{path}: {key} must be a non-empty array of tables")
    if not all(isinstance(record, dict) for record in value):
        raise PolicyError(f"{path}: every {key} entry must be a table")
    return cast(list[dict[str, object]], value)


def load_policy(root: Path = ROOT) -> Policy:
    target_path = root / TARGETS_MANIFEST
    step_path = root / STEPS_MANIFEST
    target_records = _records(_load_toml(target_path), "targets", target_path)
    step_records = _records(_load_toml(step_path), "steps", step_path)

    targets: list[Target] = []
    target_ids: set[str] = set()
    for index, record in enumerate(target_records):
        context = f"{target_path}: targets[{index}]"
        target_id = _required_string(record, "id", context)
        if target_id in target_ids:
            raise PolicyError(f"{context}.id duplicates {target_id!r}")
        target_ids.add(target_id)
        targets.append(Target(target_id, _required_string(record, "runner", context)))

    config_ids: set[str] = set()
    for config_path in sorted((root / ".mise").glob("config.*.toml")):
        document = _load_toml(config_path)
        env = document.get("env")
        if not isinstance(env, dict):
            raise PolicyError(f"{config_path}: env must be a table")
        env = cast(dict[str, object], env)
        variant = env.get("MLN_FFI_VARIANT")
        if not isinstance(variant, str):
            raise PolicyError(f"{config_path}: env.MLN_FFI_VARIANT must be a string")
        expected = config_path.name.removeprefix("config.").removesuffix(".toml")
        if variant != expected:
            raise PolicyError(
                f"{config_path}: MLN_FFI_VARIANT is {variant!r}, expected {expected!r}"
            )
        config_ids.add(variant)

    missing_targets = sorted(config_ids - target_ids)
    missing_configs = sorted(target_ids - config_ids)
    if missing_targets or missing_configs:
        details = []
        if missing_targets:
            details.append(f"missing CI targets: {', '.join(missing_targets)}")
        if missing_configs:
            details.append(f"missing mise configs: {', '.join(missing_configs)}")
        raise PolicyError("; ".join(details))

    steps: list[Step] = []
    step_ids: set[str] = set()
    covered_targets: set[str] = set()
    for index, record in enumerate(step_records):
        context = f"{step_path}: steps[{index}]"
        step_id = _required_string(record, "id", context)
        if not ID_PATTERN.fullmatch(step_id):
            raise PolicyError(f"{context}.id must match {ID_PATTERN.pattern!r}")
        if step_id in step_ids:
            raise PolicyError(f"{context}.id duplicates {step_id!r}")
        step_ids.add(step_id)

        supported = record.get("targets")
        if not isinstance(supported, list) or not supported:
            raise PolicyError(f"{context}.targets must be a non-empty string array")
        if not all(isinstance(target, str) for target in supported):
            raise PolicyError(f"{context}.targets must contain only strings")
        supported = cast(list[str], supported)
        if len(supported) != len(set(supported)):
            raise PolicyError(f"{context}.targets contains duplicates")
        unknown = sorted(set(supported) - target_ids)
        if unknown:
            raise PolicyError(
                f"{context}.targets contains unknown targets: {', '.join(unknown)}"
            )

        comment = record.get("comment")
        if comment is not None and (not isinstance(comment, str) or not comment):
            raise PolicyError(f"{context}.comment must be a non-empty string")
        covered_targets.update(supported)
        steps.append(
            Step(
                step_id,
                _required_string(record, "name", context),
                _required_string(record, "run", context),
                tuple(supported),
                comment,
            )
        )

    uncovered = sorted(target_ids - covered_targets)
    if uncovered:
        raise PolicyError(f"targets have no CI steps: {', '.join(uncovered)}")
    return Policy(tuple(targets), tuple(steps))


def render_workflow(policy: Policy) -> str:
    lines = [
        "# Generated by `mise run ci:generate`; edit .github/ci/*.toml instead.",
        "name: CI",
        "",
        "on:",
        "  push:",
        "    branches: [main]",
        "  pull_request:",
        "    branches: [main]",
        "",
        "permissions:",
        "  contents: read",
        "",
        "concurrency:",
        "  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref }}",
        "  cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}",
        "",
        "jobs:",
        "  hygiene:",
        "    name: hygiene",
        "    runs-on: ubuntu-latest",
        "    timeout-minutes: 30",
        "    steps:",
        "      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3",
        "      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps",
        "      - run: mise run ci:lint",
        "",
        "  docs:",
        "    name: docs",
        "    runs-on: ubuntu-latest",
        "    timeout-minutes: 60",
        "    steps:",
        "      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3",
        "      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps",
        "      - run: mise run //docs:build",
        "",
        "  variants:",
        "    name: variant / ${{ matrix.mise_env }}",
        "    runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}",
        "    timeout-minutes: 120",
        "    env:",
        "      MISE_ENV: ${{ matrix.mise_env }}",
        "    strategy:",
        "      fail-fast: false",
        "      matrix:",
        "        include:",
    ]
    for target in policy.targets:
        supported_steps = [
            step.id for step in policy.steps if target.id in step.targets
        ]
        lines.extend(
            [
                f"          - mise_env: {target.id}",
                f"            runner: {target.runner}",
                f"            ci_steps: [{', '.join(supported_steps)}]",
            ]
        )

    lines.extend(
        [
            "    steps:",
            "      - uses: actions/checkout@df4cb1c069e1874edd31b4311f1884172cec0e10 # v6.0.3",
            "      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup-ci-deps",
            "",
            "      - run: mise run configure",
        ]
    )
    for step in policy.steps:
        if step.comment:
            lines.append(f"      # {step.comment}")
        lines.extend(
            [
                f"      - name: {step.name}",
                f"        if: ${{{{ contains(matrix.ci_steps, '{step.id}') }}}}",
                f"        run: {step.run}",
            ]
        )

    lines.extend(
        [
            "",
            "  required:",
            "    name: ci-required",
            "    runs-on: ubuntu-latest",
            "    timeout-minutes: 5",
            "    if: ${{ always() }}",
            "    needs:",
            "      - hygiene",
            "      - docs",
            "      - variants",
            "    steps:",
            "      - name: Fail if required jobs failed",
            "        if: ${{ contains(needs.*.result, 'failure') || contains(needs.*.result, 'cancelled') || contains(needs.*.result, 'skipped') }}",
            "        run: |",
            "          echo '${{ toJSON(needs) }}'",
            "          exit 1",
            "",
            "      - run: true",
            "",
        ]
    )
    return "\n".join(lines)


def check_workflow(path: Path, generated: str) -> bool:
    existing = path.read_text() if path.exists() else ""
    if existing == generated:
        return True
    sys.stderr.writelines(
        difflib.unified_diff(
            existing.splitlines(keepends=True),
            generated.splitlines(keepends=True),
            fromfile=str(path),
            tofile=f"{path} (generated)",
        )
    )
    sys.stderr.write("CI workflow is stale; run `mise run ci:generate`.\n")
    return False


def main() -> int:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
    parser.add_argument(
        "--check", action="store_true", help="fail if the workflow is stale"
    )
    args = parser.parse_args()

    try:
        generated = render_workflow(load_policy())
    except (OSError, tomllib.TOMLDecodeError, PolicyError) as error:
        parser.error(str(error))

    workflow_path = ROOT / WORKFLOW
    if args.check:
        return 0 if check_workflow(workflow_path, generated) else 1
    workflow_path.write_text(generated)
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())
